definition: A fundamental concept in Retrieval Augmented Generation under Retrieval, representing Reranking.
simple_explanation: Think of Reranking as an essential building block of Retrieval Augmented Generation. It is commonly used within Retrieval.
technical_explanation: In production Retrieval Augmented Generation systems, Reranking plays a key role within Retrieval. Engineering implementations must account for scaling constraints and design trade-offs.
investor_explanation: Robust configuration of Reranking directly affects system efficiency and business-critical accuracy metrics, driving cost savings and higher end-user retention.
examples: We optimized Reranking in our Retrieval Augmented Generation pipeline to improve performance.
Our team discussed the differences between Reranking and alternative solutions.
